intent: A highly granular, visual project orchestration platform designed for distributed teams to manage complex workflows through a kanban-based hierarchical architecture. Its unique value proposition lies in 'Dynamic Lifecycle Linking', which allows cards to trigger automated transitions across different boards, ensuring cross-functional synchronization. The core workflow involves creating workspaces containing customizable boards, populating them with enriched cards (metadata, file attachments, and threaded comments), and enforcing role-based access controls to maintain data security during task progression.

entities.name: WorkspaceContainer
entities.description: The root logical partition that aggregates boards, project settings, and team-specific metadata to define the scope of collaboration.

entities.name: OrchestrationBoard
entities.description: A structural grouping mechanism that houses cards within defined status columns and enforces specific layout and workflow rules.

entities.name: EnrichedCard
entities.description: The atomic unit of work containing heterogeneous metadata, task specifications, and references to persistent file storage assets.

entities.name: LifecycleTriggerRule
entities.description: A conditional logic entity that monitors state transitions on an EnrichedCard to fire automated downstream events across unrelated boards.

entities.name: AccessPolicyMapping
entities.description: A granular permission object linking organizational roles to specific boards or cards to enforce fine-grained visibility controls.

entities.name: ExecutionAuditLog
entities.description: An immutable append-only ledger tracking all state mutations, user activity, and automated trigger results for compliance and debugging.

entities.name: CollaborationThread
entities.description: A structured repository of historical messaging and context-sensitive interactions anchored to a specific EnrichedCard.

architecture.description: The system follows a reactive event-driven architecture. Users interact with the React SPA, which communicates via a RESTful API and WebSocket gateway. Upon updating an EnrichedCard, the Node.js backend updates the PostgreSQL database and broadcasts the change via Socket.io. Simultaneously, the LifecycleTriggerRule engine evaluates the change; if criteria are met, it performs an asynchronous operation to create, update, or transition cards in secondary OrchestrationBoards. All file binary data is offloaded to S3 with signed URLs passed to the frontend for secure retrieval. Authentication flows via OAuth 2.0, with session state validated against the AccessPolicyMapping at each request to ensure strict operational security.

relationships: AccessPolicyMapping references various entities via target_id

validation_rules: metadata must be valid JSON object

endpoints.summary: List all OrchestrationBoards in a workspace
List all EnrichedCards in a board

rules.description: Ensure immutable integrity of ExecutionAuditLog records upon any mutation of EnrichedCard state.
rules.logic: The database transaction must include a simultaneous insert into the ExecutionAuditLog; if the audit insertion fails, the card update must rollback.

rules.description: Validate AccessPolicyMapping before processing any API request to ensure authorized entity interaction.
rules.logic: Intercept every incoming API call, resolve the user's role against the AccessPolicyMapping for the target entity ID, and deny access if scope is insufficient.

rules.description: Synchronize LifecycleTriggerRule execution post-card-update to maintain state consistency across boards.
rules.logic: Evaluate current card state against registered LifecycleTriggerRule criteria; if criteria match, execute asynchronous payload creation for targeted OrchestrationBoard.

rules.description: Enforce strict schema validation for file storage metadata references on EnrichedCards.
rules.logic: Verify that all references to S3 assets contain valid signed URL patterns and existence flags before committing the update to the PostgreSQL database.

rules.description: Ensure WorkspaceContainer isolation for cross-board operations.
rules.logic: LifecycleTriggerRule execution is blocked if the source and target boards reside in different WorkspaceContainers, unless explicit cross-workspace permissions are granted.
